title: Nephrodium decursive-pinnatum
taxonomy: Nephrodium decursive-pinnatum is a species of fern. It belongs to the family Matoniaceae. The genus Nephrodium is now considered to be a synonym of the genus Phlebopteris. Nephrodium decursive-pinnatum is therefore classified as a member of the genus Phlebopteris in the family Matoniaceae. However, further research may be required for accurate classification.

intro: Nephrodium decursive-pinnatum, also known as Decaisnea, is a plant species belonging to the family Berberidaceae, native to the Himalayan regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce inflammation, due to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium decursive-pinnatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Decaisnea is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
